title: erythroxylum sechellarum
intro: Erythroxylum sechellarum, also known as Sechellarum or Island coca, is a plant species native to the Seychelles island. It is a close relative of the coca plant (Erythroxylum coca), but unlike its cousin, it is not commonly used for its psychoactive properties. Instead, Erythroxylum sechellarum is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and lower blood pressure. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to address various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.
